topics: Nonlinear Control Systems - Introduction to nonlinear dynamics and control
Lyapunov methods and stability analysis. Optimal and Robust Control - Overview of optimal control theory
Robust control techniques and their applications. Adaptive and Learning-Based Control - Adaptive control mechanisms
CPS Security and Privacy Security Challenges in CPS - Common vulnerabilities and attack vectors
Security protocols and encryption techniques. Privacy in CPS - Privacy concerns and regulations
Privacy-preserving techniques in data collection and processing. Intrusion Detection and Response - Techniques for detecting and mitigating attacks
Earliest Deadline First (EDF). Real-Time Communication Protocols - Communication protocols in real-time CPS
Network-on-Chip (NoC) and Time-Triggered Protocols. Performance Analysis and Optimization - Techniques for performance analysis of real-time systems

Interdisciplinary Applications of CPS CPS in Healthcare - Applications and challenges in healthcare CPS CPS in Smart Cities - Smart infrastructure and urban planning. Emerging Trends and Research Overview of cutting-edge research in CPS

edge computing. Project based learning  The objective of Project based learning to strengthen the understanding of student’s fundamentals through effective application of theoretical concepts. Projects can help to boost student’s skills and widen the horizon of their thinking.  The ultimate aim of an engineering student is to resolve a problem by applying theoretical knowledge.  In the beginning

students shall be given an awareness and/or guideline on how to implement and learn this course through a project and form groups in the first few weeks.  Each group should identify a project that can be done to understand a part of the contents of this course and perform a literature survey.  Choose a suitable platform for your application and the input output devices to be interfaced.  Develop the block diagram and present before the jury within first month after the commencement of classes.  Implement necessary hardware and software to develop the prototype.  Prepare a report and present before the jury for final evaluation
